span.font: Encapsulation: - By name only we can understand, encapsulating data like attributes and methods within a single unit (the object), and hiding the internal details from outside world
Abstraction: - hiding the complexity from the end user, and providing easier method for dynamically calculated values, so that user don't need to calculate
Polymorphism: - same logic/method working for different thing in a same way but returns the different dynamic value
Inheritance: - Means accessing the same properties/methods from another

div.font: Variables declaration and initialization in class: -

li.font: In Dynamic language, Variable declaration is not needed but in static language field declaration is needed even for constructor args. (to know reason refer ce.svg)

In Dynamic language, Bydefault Variable initialization is undefined, while in most static language string = null, integer = 0 and boolean = false, object reference = null. In some languages explicit initialization may also be needed

li.font: A class method can only access class properties
but Instance method can access both class and instance properties

div.font: In both the cases, static and non-static, private property cannot be accessed outside the class
but public property can be accessed in both within and outside of the class

li.font: Static properties can only be accessed using class name itself withing and outside of the class
And Instance properties can only be accessed using that specific instance name only, and using "this" keyword within the class
